narrative_summary:
The BRCA2 missense variant p.Ser2533Cys (c.7598C>G) occurs in the region of the gene implicated in hereditary breast and ovarian cancer susceptibility. Clinical interpretation of this change has been addressed in recent studies.

A 2022 analysis that incorporated homology‑directed repair functional assay results into an ACMG/AMP framework classified the variant as likely benign (LBEN), supporting a non‑pathogenic role for p.Ser2533Cys [PMC9433957].

Experimental assessment of the variant’s effect on splicing was attempted in a 2019 minigene study of BRCA2 exons 14‑15, but mutagenesis of c.7598C>G failed, preventing functional testing; only in silico splice‑site predictions were reported and no pathogenicity classification was assigned [PMC6546720].

Computational splice‑site analysis for c.7598C>G indicated alterations to a 5′ splice‑site and changes in exonic splicing enhancer motifs, but the study did not draw a definitive conclusion regarding its impact on splicing or disease risk [PMC6546720].

Taken together, the available evidence includes a formal likely‑benign clinical classification and a lack of experimental functional data, with computational predictions suggesting possible splice‑site effects but without corroborating functional validation. The preponderance of evidence therefore supports the interpretation of BRCA2 p.Ser2533Cys as a likely benign variant.

mentions.quoted_text: A total of 52 variants were genetically engineered in the wt MGBR2_14-20 by using specific primers (Supplementary Table S2). Despite 53 variants were initially selected, mutagenesis experiments did not work for c.**7598C > G** variant. The 52 mutant minigenes were checked by Sanger sequencing and assayed in MCF-7 cells. Results showed that 12 of them (23%) altered splicing (Table 2 and Figure 3), seven of which had previously been classified as pathogenic and five as VUS (Table 3). Among these 12 variants, there were 9 intronic, 2 missense and 1 nonsense changes. Functionally, the 9 intronic variants (c.7008-3C > G, c.7008-2A > T, c.7008-1G > A, c.7435+1G > C, c.7436-2A > T, c.7436-1G > A, c.7617+1G > A, c.7617+1G > T, and c.7617+2T > G) disrupted the natural splice sites, the two missense changes (c.7177A > G and c.7447A > G) triggered the use of de novo splice sites and originated other transcripts, and the nonsense one (c.7501C > T) probably altered SREs despite it was primarily selected because of the creation of a new 5'ss (Table 1). According to, nine variants induced severe splicing disruptions as they produced more than 60% of aberrant transcripts, ranging from 92.8 to 100% (Table 3).

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study evaluated spliceogenic BRCA2 variants in exons 14‑15, but the S2533C (c.7598C>G) variant could not be experimentally tested and only computational splice‑site predictions are reported, without a pathogenicity classification.
article_mentions.title: Minigene Splicing Assays Identify 12 Spliceogenic Variants of BRCA2 Exons 14 and 15

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: Using HDR functional assay data within an ACMG/AMP framework, the study re‑classified BRCA2 S2533C as likely benign (LBEN).
article_mentions.title: Classification of BRCA2 variants of uncertain significance (VUS) using an ACMG/AMP model incorporating a homology directed repair (HDR) functional assay
